meta.title: Applied Fiction: Training That Engages The Team
meta.description:
Why you should watch this:

- Initial Training Stats: 36% Disengaged, 60% Not Engaged and ONLY 4% ENGAGED  (at 4:35)

- After Applied Fiction Training: 4% Disengaged, 13% Not Engaged and 83% Engaged (at 28:55)

Yes that's a change from 4% to 83% Engaged in the training.

Lisa Carrington of Arizona Public Service (APS) had a challenge of training 2,000' people on NERC CIP / ICS cyber security. And the results of the current security training, which are typical for the industry, were dreadful.

Lisa brought in Christopher Lazarro and the team from MetaMythic to use applied fiction to create an online training program that would actively engage 2,000 employees that needed training. The trainee becomes the hero of the online story / adventure.

See some great snippets of the training beginning at 15:55. This includes physical access control, USB/removable media, and what a firewall is.

At 27:40 Lisa talks about how they moved from the 10 question test at the end of the training to measuring the effectiveness of the training throughout the entire adventure.

We also appreciate Chris and Lisa's explanation of what makes S4 unique at 0:40.
